notes: Paradigm-Shift Finder Run 1 — mechanism validation on the 5 academic colloquium transcripts reused from TARI run_002. These are NOT the intended input distribution for paradigm_shift_finder (tech-leader vision content); they are the immediately-available 2024-2026 transcripts. Expect MV-1..MV-5 success criteria but not S-1.

transcripts.speaker: Belinda Li
transcripts.speaker_affiliation: MIT EECS (PhD student, advisor Jacob Andreas)
transcripts.topic: Building AI systems with coherent and updatable models of the world, user, and themselves
transcripts.claude_cutoff_relationship: Talk timing overlaps Claude knowledge cutoff (Jan 2026).

transcripts.speaker: Yu Sun
transcripts.topic: Test-time training and adaptive computation at inference

transcripts.speaker: Nicholas Roberts
transcripts.topic: Science of foundation models for science

transcripts.speaker: Valerie Chen
transcripts.claude_cutoff_relationship: Overlaps cutoff. Sparse atom yield expected.

transcripts.speaker: Amrith Setlur
transcripts.topic: Training models to adapt at test time via reinforcement learning

mechanism_validation_targets: MV-1: 6 layers run end-to-end without crash
MV-2: Layer 2 produces >=1 atom of each new type, or documents 0 for any type
MV-3: Layer 5 rejects >=1 candidate by RAG_UNGROUNDED or UNSTABLE_FIRST_PRINCIPLES
MV-4: Layer 6 produces market verdicts with citations
MV-5: impact_label_logger produces labeling prompt for top-3 candidates
expected_failure_modes: Academic transcripts under-produce 'prediction' atoms (researchers report past work, not vision).
Without a search cache, Layer 5 RAG layer rejects all candidates (RAG_UNGROUNDED).
Without a search cache, Layer 6 trivially marks all candidates SURVIVES_MARKET_CHECK (false negative).
Run 1 is therefore not a substantive test of S-1; it is a smoke test of the 6 layers.
